skill.name: azure-cosmos-rust
skill.description: Guides Rust developers through Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL API setup, authentication, client hierarchy, document operations, patching, and SDK reference links.
skill.summary: Build Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL workflows with the Rust SDK.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network URLs, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found documentation examples only. No executable scripts, prompt injection, credential harvesting, malicious network calls, or weak cryptographic implementation were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Shell Examples
low_findings.description: The static command findings point to fenced Markdown examples showing cargo add commands. The skill does not define scripts, hooks, or instructions that execute commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious text is inside Markdown code fences and is normal Rust dependency installation guidance. No executable wrapper or dynamic command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Public Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The network findings are Azure endpoint placeholders and public reference links. No code sends data to those URLs from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static documentation references or placeholders for a user's own Cosmos DB account. There is no request code or exfiltration path in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary words in documentation, including description text and Serialize or Deserialize examples. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain Markdown metadata and Rust serde serialization terms. They do not reference MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic operations.

content.user_title: Build Azure Cosmos DB Rust Apps
content.value_statement: Rust developers often need exact SDK patterns for Cosmos DB NoSQL operations. This skill provides focused guidance for clients, authentication, documents, partition keys, and references.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to install azure_data_cosmos and azure_identity for Rust projects.
Shows how to create a CosmosClient with DeveloperToolsCredential.
Describes database and container client relationships in the Rust SDK.
Provides examples for create, read, replace, patch, and delete item operations.
Highlights partition key usage for point reads and writes.
Links to official API, source, and crate references.
limitations: Covers the Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL API only.
Does not include production deployment or infrastructure templates.
Does not validate credentials, account settings, or live database connectivity.
Does not cover every SDK option or advanced query pattern.

use_cases.title: Create a Rust Cosmos DB Prototype
use_cases.description: Use the skill to assemble the first client setup and document CRUD flow.

use_cases.title: Review SDK Usage Patterns
use_cases.description: Check whether code uses client hierarchy, partition keys, and typed models correctly.

use_cases.title: Plan Azure NoSQL Integration
use_cases.description: Use the examples to estimate implementation steps for a Rust service using Cosmos DB.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up an Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL client in Rust using this skill.

prompt_templates.title: Write CRUD Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline create, read, replace, patch, and delete operations for a Rust document type.

prompt_templates.title: Check Partition Key Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Rust Cosmos DB approach for correct partition key handling and typed deserialization.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Service Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help design a Rust service layer for Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL using reusable clients and Entra ID authentication.

output_examples.input: I need to connect a Rust app to Cosmos DB.
output_examples.output: A concise setup plan covering dependencies, DeveloperToolsCredential, CosmosClient creation, and database or container client access.

output_examples.input: I need examples for document updates.
output_examples.output: A guided explanation of replace versus patch operations, including when each approach is appropriate.

output_examples.input: I want to review my Cosmos DB Rust design.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering partition keys, reusable clients, typed models, Entra ID authentication, and SDK reference points.

best_practices: Use Entra ID authentication before key-based authentication when possible.
Always model and pass the correct partition key for point operations.
Reuse Cosmos DB client instances across requests when your application design allows it.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode production account keys in source files or prompts.
Do not omit partition keys for point reads and writes.
Do not create a new client for every single document operation.

faq.question: Which Cosmos DB API does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL API through the Rust azure_data_cosmos crate.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows DeveloperToolsCredential and recommends Entra ID before key-based authentication.

faq.question: Can it help with document CRUD operations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes create, read, replace, patch, and delete operation patterns.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only contains Markdown examples and reference guidance.

faq.question: Does it cover production operations?
faq.answer: Only partly. It does not cover monitoring, backup strategy, throughput planning, or infrastructure deployment.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
